mechanism_narrative: Growth in real retail and food services sales captures strengthening consumer demand that lifts corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months with month-over-month RSAFS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.0 percent sustains restrictive financial conditions that raise equity discount rates and compress risk asset valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in nonfarm payrolls and increases in the unemployment rate signal a coordinated labor market deterioration that reduces household income and corporate earnings forecasts simultaneously, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in over 50% of observations where PAYEMS declines and UNRATE rises jointly.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary policy regimes, a month-over-month decline in industrial production confirms that elevated interest rates are suppressing real economic activity, amplifying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO declines while FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of months when CPIAUCSL accelerates month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.3 million units signal a contracting construction sector and weakening household wealth effects that reduce aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when HOUST is below 1.3 million.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate over a three-month horizon indicates a slackening labor market that reduces consumer spending growth and corporate top-line revenue, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of quarters when UNRATE rises over a three-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing activity and higher capacity utilization that supports operating leverage and corporate profitability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when INDPRO grows month-over-month.
